Contact Us

Grand Canyon National Park Operations Update

 
 

North Rim - Current Status and Closures

Today's Weather Forecast and Webcam Views

 

Entrance Fees and Passes

Reservations are not required to enter Grand Canyon National Park. The park does not have timed entry.

What pass should you pick? Standard or annual pass? How much do they cost? Do you qualify for a military, senior, or access pass? Details

 

Grand Canyon Village (South Rim)

Visitor Services - Hours of Operation

  • Visiting for the day in an RV, or pulling a trailer? On the South Rim there are 3 designated parking lots for RVs. Details
  • Park once and ride the free Shuttle Buses around the village and out to scenic overlooks. Bus Schedules

South Rim Visitor Center Schedule

  • Daily, 9 am to 5 pm (March 21 - April 18, 2026)
  • Daily, 9 am to 4 pm (April 19 - May 2, 2026--shortened hours to accommodate seasonal training)
  • Daily, 8 am to 4 pm beginning May 3, 2026
    Trip planning and hiking information is available through permanent exhibits and sidewalk signs outside of the visitor center building.
  • Grand Canyon Conservancy Park Store (across the plaza from the Visitor Center) is open daily 8 am to 7 pm:
    • Passport To Your National Parks® stamps
    • Junior Ranger books/badges
Today's Calendar of Programs and Events.
 

Car and RV Campgrounds

NOTE: Grand Canyon National Park remains in stage 1 fire restrictions year-round which requires that a campfire always be in a designated fire ring within a maintained campground. Inner canyon backcountry users can use gas cook stoves, but campfires and other open fires are never allowed below the canyon rim.

  • Mather Campground (No Hook-ups) OPEN ALL YEAR Grand Canyon Village.
    The Mather Campground office kiosk - at the entrance to the campground is open. Reservations are recommended between March 1, and November 30, 2025 and may be made up to 6 months in advance. There are 15, first come, first served campsites that go on sale at the campground office at 8 am each day. Details

  • Trailer Village RV Campground is OPEN ALL YEAR (Full Hook-ups)

  • Camper Services (laundry and showers) at the entrance to Mather Campground

  • Desert View Campground (No Hook-ups) CLOSED for the Season
    Due to high demand, Desert View Campground now requires advanced reservations. Book reservations up to 6 months in advance for the 2026 season (Saturday, April 11, through Sunday, October 18, 2026) Details.

  • North Rim Campground (No Hook-ups) CLOSED
    The North Rim Campground is currently closed and is expected to reopen in 2026 for tent and RV camping (no hookups) once conditions allow. Details

 

Village Greenway and Canyon Rim Trails

  • Rim Trails: The Canyon Rim Trail and the GreenwayTrail system on the South Rim, are open to the public outside of the village residential area.
 

Backcountry Information and Trail Closures

Hikers and runners, visit our Key Hiking Messages page for:

  • Today's Critical Updates
  • Location and Trail Closures Map
  • Drinking Water Availability
  • Inner Canyon Weather Forecast and Heat Risk
  • NOTE: Backcountry Permits are required to camp overnight inside the canyon.
 
 

Free Shuttle Bus - Routes in Operation

Spring 2026 Schedules - March 1, through May 22, 2026

  • Village (Blue) Route Shuttle Schedule >
    First bus 7:00 am —Last bus is at 9:00 pm.
    ► Be at a bus stop no later than 8:45 pm.
    Connects the Visitor Center with lodges, campgrounds, the Backcountry Information Center, and Market Plaza.

  • Kaibab Rim (Orange) Route Schedule >
    First bus is at 6:00 am — last bus is one hour after sunset.
    Provides transportation from the Visitor Center Shuttle Bus Terminal to South Kaibab Trailhead, Yaki Point, Pipe Creek Overlook, Mather Point and Yavapai Geology Museum.

Need access to or from the South Kaibab Trailhead when the shuttle buses are not running? Contact Xanterra Taxi Service at (928) 638-2631.

  • Hikers' Express Shuttle Schedule >
    Early morning bus between the village and South Kaibab Trailhead.
    • Departs Bright Angel Lodge at 6:00 am, 7:00 am and 8:00 am.
    • Backcountry Information Center at 6:10 am, 7:10 am and 8:10 am.
    • Visitor Center Shuttle Bus Terminal at 6:20 am, 7:20 am and 8:20 am.

  • Hermits Rest (Red) Route Schedule >
    First bus is at 8:00 am — last bus is one hour after sunset.
    Starts in the village Historic District, then travels along a 7 mile (11 km) scenic road with outstanding views of Grand Canyon. Round trip takes 80 minutes (without getting off the bus).

  • Tusayan (Purple) Route - Park & Ride > Not in operation
    Service between the gateway town of Tusayan and the Visitor Center.
    ONLY IN OPERATION DURING THE SUMMER

 

South Rim Visitor Centers and Information Desks

  • South Rim Visitor Center
    • Daily, 9 am - 5 pm (December 7, 2025 - April 18, 2026)
    • Trip planning and hiking information is available through permanent exhibits and sidewalk signs outside of the visitor center building.
  • Grand Canyon Conservancy's Park Store 8 am to 7 pm daily
    (Across the plaza from the Visitor Center)
    Information Desk, Passport Stamp Station, Junior Ranger Books and Badges.
  • Backcountry Information Center - 8 am to 5 pm daily; closed 12 to 1 pm for lunch
    Near Maswik Lodge in the Village
    Backcountry permits and information.
  • Yavapai Geology Museum: 8 am to 7 pm daily
    Geology exhibits, information, and a museum shop.
  • Kolb Studio: 8 am to 7 pm daily
    Park Store on the upper floor of the studio. Exhibits downstairs.
    Guided Tours of the Kolb house are being offered by Grand Canyon Conservancy.
  • Verkamps Visitor Center: 8 am to 7 pm daily
    Exhibits, information and museum shop
  • Desert View and the park's East Entrance
    23 miles (37 km) east of Grand Canyon Village on State Route 64, Desert View Drive
  • Desert View Watchtower Park Store (View Room) 8 am to 6 pm daily
    The upper floors of the tower are open, when staffing allows, from 9 am to 4 pm. The last tower access is at 5 pm, with the stairs closed for the day at 5:30 pm. Capacity is 25 people in the tower at one time. A free ticket system admits 25 people with a 20 minute time limit.
 

South Rim Lodging and Visitor Services

Canyon Adventures - Bike Tours and Café

Grab & Go Café, Coffee Bar, Bicycle Rental and Tours
(across the plaza from the Visitor Center)
Bike Rentals: daily, 8 am to 5 pm
Café: daily, 8 am to 5 pm

Grand Canyon National Park Lodges (Xanterra)

El Tovar Hotel

  • Dining Room--Advanced reservations are required for lunch and dinner at the El Tovar Dining Room. Make online reservations here
    • Breakfast: 6:30 am to 10:00 am
    • Lunch: 11:00 am to 2:30 pm
    • Dinner: 4:30 pm to 9:30 pm
    • Dinner Reservations are recommended and available 45-days in advance online at
      https://www.grandcanyonlodges.com/dining/el-tovar-dining-room-and-lounge/
  • El Tovar Lounge: 11:00 am to 9:30 pm (food until 8:30 pm)
  • El Tovar Patio and Beer Garden: 12:00 pm to dusk (weather permitting)

Bright Angel Lodge

  • Bright Angel Lodge — Canyonside Coffee Bar/Fountain: daily, 6:00 am to 6:00 pm
  • Bright Angel Lodge — Fred Harvey Tavern: 11:00 am to 9:00 pm daily (food until 8:30 pm)
  • Bright Angel Lodge — Harvey House Café
    • Breakfast: 6:30 am to 10:00 am
    • Lunch: 11:00 am to 4:00 pm
    • Dinner: 4:00 pm to 9:00 pm
  • Bright Angel Lodge — Arizona Steak House:
    • Lunch: 11:30 am to 3:30 pm
    • Dinner: 4:30 pm to 9:00 pm
    • Dinner Reservations are recommended and available 45-days in advance online at https://www.grandcanyonlodges.com/dine/arizona-room/

Maswik Lodge

  • Maswik Lodge Front Desk: 7 am to 11 pm daily
  • Maswik Lodge Gift Shop: 8 am to 8 pm daily
  • Maswik Lodge Food Court and Grab & Go
    • Breakfast: 6:00 am to 10:30 am
    • All-day Menu: 10:30 am to 9:00 pm
  • Maswik Lodge Pizza Pub: 12:00 pm to 9:00 pm
    • First come, First Serve; Dine in Only. Serving food until 8:30 pm

Other Locations

  • Hopi House Gift Shop: 9 am to 6 pm daily
  • Lookout Studio Gift Shop: CLOSED for refurbishing (reopening March 2026)
  • Hermits Rest Gift Shop: 9 am to 5 pm daily
  • Phantom Ranch Cabins only and limited canteen service Details

  • Mule Rides - Details

  • Taxi Service: (24 hours) 928-638-2631
    Between South Kaibab Trailhead and Village
    Between Village and     Tusayan/ Airport
    Not serving Hermit Road at this time

  • Garage Services:
    Mechanical services for cars, trucks, or RVs may be obtained at the Xanterra Public Garage, located on the main park loop road, near the Grand Canyon Railway Depot and just east of the Xanterra General Office Building. The Garage is open daily from 8:00 a.m. to noon and 1:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m. Call the garage through Xanterra's central switchboard at 928-638-2631.

  • Guided Bus Tours - Take the time to learn some of the captivating history and geology of this timeless location. All In-Park tours feature a trained driver-guide, who will provide an informative narrative throughout your tour of the park. Details

  • Grand Canyon Kennel: 7:30 am to 5 pm, Daily . Details
    Overnight boarding is available seven days/week.

    Visit Grand Canyon National Park Lodges' Website

Grand Canyon Railway & Hotel (in Williams, Arizona)

 

Delaware North — South Rim Village

  • Yavapai Lodge: Lodging - 24 hours
  • Yavapai Lodge Coffee Shop/Cafe: 7 am to 2 pm - daily
  • Yavapai Lodge Tavern and Patio: 11 am to 9 pm - daily
  • Yavapai Lodge Gift Shop: 8 am to 8 pm - daily
  • Yavapai Dining Hall
    • Breakfast: 7 am to 10 am
    • Dinner: 5 pm to 8 pm
  • Canyon Village Market and General Store: 7 am to 8 pm - daily
  • Canyon Village Market Deli: 7 am to 5:30 pm - daily
  • Trailer Village RV Campground —Office Kiosk: 10 am to 6 pm daily

  • Camper Services building: laundry and showers
    7 am to 6 pm daily. Last laundry load: 5 pm — Last shower: 5:45 pm

  • Visit Delaware North's Website >

 

Services at Market Plaza

  • ATM Machines are available at Canyon Village Market and Yavapai Lodge.
  • Grand Canyon Post Office window service open 8:30 am to 3:30 pm, Monday through Friday. Closed Saturday and Sunday.
  • Canyon Village Market and Deli (General Store)
  • Yavapai Lodge Lodging, Cafe, Restaurant, Tavern, Gift Shop.

Grand Canyon Clinic

North Country Healthcare (1 Clinic Road)

Urgent Care - Family Practice - Physical Therapy & Virtual Visits

  • Memorial Day to Labor Day:
    9 am to 6 pm daily (Summer)
  • Labor Day to Memorial Day:
    Monday through Friday, 8 am to 5 pm (Fall, Winter, Spring)

Pharmacy:

  • Hours vary based on staffing. Please call ahead at 928-638-2551.

The clinic offers walk-ins and same day appointments for routine and sick visits.

Please call ahead at 928-638-2551 to schedule an appointment

 

Desert View is Open

Desert View is a small settlement on the South Rim located 23 miles/ 37 km east of Grand Canyon Village, and near the eastern edge of Grand Canyon. Arizona State Route 64, also known as Desert View Drive, is a 23 mile (37 km) scenic road that connects Desert View with Grand Canyon Village. Desert View Drive is open to all vehicles throughout the year. (Unless temporarily closed by snow)

  • The East Entrance to the park at Desert View is open 24/7.
    Available Visitor Services

  • Desert View Campground (no hook-ups) CLOSED
    Due to high demand, Desert View Campground requires advanced reservations.
    Book reservations up to 6 months in advance for the 2026 season
    (Saturday, April 11, through Sunday, October 18, 2026) Details

  • Learn about touring 23 mile (37 km) scenic Desert View Drive in your vehicle: Details

 

North Rim

  • North Rim - Current Status and Closures
    The North Rim of Grand Canyon National Park will reopen, including Highway 67, the Cape Royal Road, and Point Imperial Roads on May 15, 2026. See link above for more detailed information on North Rim status and conditions.

 

Public Health Updates

  • July 12, 2025 - Coconino County health officials say two people have died from hantavirus in the last 15 months.Details
  • July 11, 2025 - Northern Arizona Healthcare and Coconino County officials confirmed a person died of pneumonic plague at the Flagstaff Medical Center. Details
 

Havasupai Visitor Information

For 2026 campground and lodge reservation information, visit: www.havasupaireservations.com —This website is owned and operated by the Tribe.

Also:: Official Havasupai Tribe Website (theofficialhavasupaitribe.com)

The Tribe has an official Facebook tourism page > Havasupai Tribe Tourism | Facebook

There are countless Havasupai-related pages on Facebook, however, these are not operated or authorized by the Tribe. Please visit and like their tourism page where official information regarding tourism can be found.

 

Visiting the Navajo Nation

  • Navajo Tribal Parks are open and welcoming visitors. For more information about Navajo Tribal Parks, call: 928-871-6647 or visit www.navajonationparks.org
